title: Tristagma felipponei
taxonomy: Tristagma felipponei belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to South America and is a bulbous perennial that produces white or cream-colored flowers. The classification of Tristagma felipponei is further detailed as a member of the subfamily Allioideae. The genus Tristagma is part of the tribe Gilliesieae. As a result, Tristagma felipponei is classified as a member of the Amaryllidaceae family.

This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues.
